title: salix lasiandra subsp. lyallii
intro: Salix lasiandra subsp. lyallii, also known as Lyall's willow, is a subspecies of the Black willow tree native to the western coast of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial in treating various health conditions. The bark and leaves of Lyall's willow contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, making it a natural remedy for pain relief, reducing fever, and alleviating symptoms of rheumatism. Herbal preparations of Lyall's willow include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from colds and flu to menstrual cramps and digestive problems.
